intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer gentle, approachable interfaces over sharp geometric forms
facade.axisTargeted: Roundness vs. sharpness in UI elements
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Design system should favor curves in buttons, cards, and containers to feel welcoming and less clinical
facade.rejectImplies: Design system should prioritize clean lines and sharper corners for a more precise, data-focused aesthetic

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer understated data visualization over colorful charts for health tracking, suggesting a calm, analytical mood
facade.label: Minimal line graphs
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard favors restraint, clarity, and focus—thin lines, monochrome, whitespace. Visual priority on *data*, not decoration.
facade.rejectImplies: Users want more visual energy, color coding, or expressive design—moving toward playful, vibrant, or densely-layered aesthetics.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer warm, conversational language over clinical health terminology
facade.axisTargeted: voice and personality: friendly vs professional
facade.label: Casual check-in tone
facade.acceptImplies: Adopt colloquial, encouraging voice ('How'd it go?' instead of 'Data entry required'). Use emoji sparingly. Feel like a supportive friend.
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward formal, medical-grade language. Clinical labels. Objective data framing. Doctor's office energy.

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer visual separation between navigation and data, with high contrast between dense UI and readable metric display
facade.label: Dark sidebar, light content
facade.acceptImplies: User values defined zones, clear distinction between controls and information, likely prefers sidebar navigation for health dashboards
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer unified color schemes, floating navigation, or tab-based layouts; may find high contrast between sections visually jarring or prefer softer boundaries

evidence.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer gentle, approachable interfaces over sharp geometric forms
evidence.implication: Design system should favor curves in buttons, cards, and containers to feel welcoming and less clinical

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer immediate, click-based logging over gesture-based entry for health tracking, valuing speed over fluid interaction
facade.axisTargeted: MOTION AND BEHAVIOR — instant click vs animated gesture
facade.label: Instant data snap
facade.acceptImplies: Users want frictionless, tap-to-log interfaces. Animations may feel like overhead when tracking daily habits. Snappy feedback beats fluid transitions.
facade.rejectImplies: Users value the ritual and intentionality of gesture-based entry. Fluid, animated logging makes tracking feel more mindful and less mechanical. Speed is secondary to feeling present in the act.

facade.hypothesis: Users building health dashboards oscillate between wanting prominent key metrics vs. wanting those metrics embedded in narrative context (trends, comparisons, goals). This probe tests whether the user prefers isolated, scannable data or data that answers 'why should I care right now?'
facade.axisTargeted: INFORMATION DENSITY & NARRATIVE FRAMING — isolated metrics vs. contextualized data
facade.label: Big numbers, small context
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward clean, fast scanning. Wants a glanceable dashboard with prominent KPIs (weight, sleep hours, workout count) without interpretation layered in. Prefers visual hierarchy over storytelling.
facade.rejectImplies: User wants data to mean something immediately. Prefers metrics paired with insight (e.g., 'Sleep: 7h — up from 6.2h last week' or 'Workouts: 4 — on track for weekly goal'). Wants the dashboard to narrate, not just display.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer a single-color scheme with tonal variations over multi-color accent systems, reducing visual noise and supporting sustained focus on data patterns
facade.label: Monochrome palette
facade.acceptImplies: Design system should use grayscale + single accent color max; metrics displayed through tone/saturation shifts rather than hue variety
facade.rejectImplies: Users want color diversity in the interface; consider chromatic accent system for different experiment types or metric categories
